(2 comments)

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

Mike - is this patch temporary? Because it didn't implement what Oleg wants for selective ibits.

Patch Set 7:

Jinshan, as I can see it does, trybits are granted selectively, so if any can be granted - it is granted, not just 'all or none'. This is main idea of selective trybits. Original trybits were granted only immediately and all or not granted at all, with this patch they are also granted only immediately but selectively.
Adding them to the waiting queue is the further optimization which is separated now just to check both changes separately.

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

I see. So you will address this in the 2nd phase of the patch. That's fine. Could you please revise the patch one more time and move the code of handling try_bits closer in ldlm_inodebits_compat_queue()?
Uploaded patch set 8.

Patch Set 11:

Thanks for the replies.

We're probably going to just do the simple thing of undoing this one line change at Cray, but it would obviously be good to get that or Oleg's patch in for 2.12... It's quite a serious perf regression in some cases.

Patch Set 11:

> Thanks for the replies.
 > 
 > We're probably going to just do the simple thing of undoing this
 > one line change at Cray, but it would obviously be good to get that
 > or Oleg's patch in for 2.12... It's quite a serious perf regression
 > in some cases.

Patrick, it would be more useful if you submit your patch to master so that it can revert this regression.  It sounds less complex/risky than Oleg's patch, and does not preclude his patch from landing at some later point.

While this is a bit of work for you to submit (though not very much, since you already have a patch), it is also unfair to us to assume that we will do all of the fixing on master and you can just cherry pick what you want onto your 2.11-based branch.

We don't get funded by OpenSFS to do tree maintenance for a long time already, nor by Cray, so if the "community of Lustre" is to survive it needs more than one organization doing the majority of the ongoing maintenance work.

Patch Set 11:

Andreas - Of course, I'm happy to open an LU and push it, since it's desired.  It's hardly any work at all - I only didn't *already* because Oleg replied indicating he had something in flight.

I was just offering my thoughts on what's needed for the next release.  I'm happy to help provide it, if my contribution is needed/desired.

I'll push it a bit later today.

commit.subject: LU-10269 ldlm: fix the issues introduced by try bits
commit.message:
LU-10269 ldlm: fix the issues introduced by try bits

Try to grant optinal try_bits if lock is not blocked
and to be granted immediately otherwise trybits are cleared,
therefore there are no locks with trybits in both granted and
waiting queue.

Also mdt_object_lock_try() is changed to don't return negative for
try lock only case.

Test-Parameters: mdscount=1 mdtcount=1 testlist=racer,racer,racer
Signed-off-by: Mikhal Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Change-Id: If3f5734355511ad1af71d5996026d04c60f3e8c1
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30246
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
